heading: Procedures to determine presumptions of service connection based on toxic exposure; definitions
label: 1171 Procedures to determine presumptions of service connection based on toxic exposure; definitions
notes: Amendments2025—Subsec. (b)(2). amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) defined the term “Individual Longitudinal Exposure Record”.Pub. L. 119–60

Correction of Exposure Records by Members of the Armed Forces and Veterans, , , provided that:Pub. L. 117–168, title VIII, § 803136 Stat. 1802Aug. 10, 2022“(a).—In GeneralThe Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall coordinate with the Secretary of Defense to provide a means for veterans to update their records as necessary to reflect exposures to occupational or environmental hazards by such member or veteran in the Individual Longitudinal Exposure Record.“(b)Evidence.—“(1).—Provision of evidenceTo update a record under subsection (a), a veteran shall provide such evidence as the Secretary of Veterans Affairs considers necessary.“(2).—RegulationsThe Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall prescribe by regulation the evidence considered necessary under paragraph (1).“(c).—DefinitionsIn this section:“(1).—Individual longitudinal exposure recordThe term ‘Individual Longitudinal Exposure Record’ has the meaning given such term in , United States Code, as added by section 202.section 1171 of title 38“(2).—Toxic exposureThe term ‘toxic exposure’ has the meaning given such term in , United States Code, as amended by section 102(b).”section 101 of title 38
amendments: Amendments2025—Subsec. (b)(2). amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) defined the term “Individual Longitudinal Exposure Record”.Pub. L. 119–60

children.label: i
children.text: public notice regarding what formal evaluations the Secretary plans to conduct; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the public an opportunity to comment on the proposed formal evaluations;

children.label: i
children.text: advice to the Secretary on toxic-exposed veterans and cases in which veterans who, during active military, naval, air, or space service, may have experienced a toxic exposure or their dependents may have experienced a toxic exposure while the veterans were serving in the active military, naval, air, or space service;

children.label: ii
children.text: recommendations to the Secretary on corrections needed in the Individual Longitudinal Exposure Record to better reflect veterans and dependents described in clause (i); and

children.label: iii
children.text: recommendations to the Secretary regarding which cases of possible toxic exposure should be reviewed;

the Secretary provides for formal evaluations of such recommendations under and takes into account reports received by the Secretary from the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine under ; andsection 1173 of this titlesection 1176 of this title
the Secretary issues regulations under .section 1174 of this title
The term “illness” includes a disease or other condition affecting the health of an individual, including mental and physical health.
The term “Individual Longitudinal Exposure Record” means the data system maintained under .section 996 of title 10
